title_en: Prepare for a world of ad hoc coalitions
title_zh: 为临时联盟世界做好准备
rubric_en: Neither wishful multilateralism nor cold realpolitik will do. Mark Carney and Alexander Stubb suggest a third way
rubric_zh: 既非一厢情愿的多边主义，也非冷冰冰的现实政治——马克·卡尼与亚历山大·斯图布提出第三条路径

paragraphs.en: WE LEAD TWO countries that do not have the luxury of relying on old orders of geography. Finland shares a border with a Russia that is waging a war of aggression against Ukraine and actively interfering in other societies instead of reforming its own. Canada shares the world’s longest land border with a United States that is reassessing its priorities and transforming all its commercial relationships.
paragraphs.zh: 我们代表着两个不能依赖旧有地缘格局的国家。芬兰与俄罗斯接壤，而俄罗斯正对乌克兰发动侵略战争，并且在干涉其他社会，而不是改革自身。加拿大与美国接壤，而美国拥有世界上最长的陆地边界，并且正在重新评估其优先事项，重塑其全部商业关系。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, we are striking closer relations with powers like India and, while avoiding strategic dependencies, China, as well as with countries of the Global South, because power no longer exclusively sits at a single Western centre. Power is spread across three groupings: a Global West, a Global East and a Global South, each increasingly autonomous. Truly global trends, including power over compute, data and artificial-intelligence models, are spawning a fourth, technology-driven grouping, shaping how we live, work and connect—unconstrained by borders and accountable to few rules.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，我们正在与印度等力量加强关系，并在避免战略依赖的前提下，与中国以及全球南方国家保持更紧密的互动，因为权力不再只集中在单一的西方中心。权力正在分布于三个阵营：全球西方、全球东方和全球南方，而且每个阵营都越来越自主。真正具有全球性的趋势，包括对算力、数据和人工智能模型的控制，正在催生第四个以技术为驱动的阵营，它在塑造我们的生活、工作和连接方式——这种方式不受国界约束，也几乎不受规则约束。

paragraphs.en: The world order built after 1945 is unravelling. Institutions as they stand are ill-prepared to avert this decline. This is a moment that comes once in a century, in which our collective prosperity is defined by what we choose to build, and how we build it.
paragraphs.zh: 1945年后建立的世界秩序正在解体。现有机构毫无准备，无法阻止这种衰落。这是一个百年难遇的时刻，我们共同的繁荣将由我们选择建设什么、以及如何建设来决定。

paragraphs.en: The work ahead requires neither wishful multilateralism, which only defends the status quo, nor cold realpolitik that dismantles it altogether. The alternative is values-based realism. A new approach that is both principled and pragmatic. Principled in its commitment to fundamental values of freedom, human rights, sustainability and solidarity. Pragmatic in recognising that not every partner will share all of our values. Put simply, we must form calibrated alliances, integrating deeply with those countries that share our values, and remain open-eyed with those that do not.
paragraphs.zh: 未来的工作既不需要只维护现状的一厢情愿的多边主义，也不需要把秩序彻底拆解的冷酷现实主义。替代方案是以价值为基础的现实主义。一种兼具原则性与务实性的全新路径。其原则性体现在坚持自由、人权、可持续性和团结这些根本价值上。其务实性体现在承认并非每一个伙伴都会共享我们所有的价值观。简单地说，我们必须建立经过精心校准的联盟，与那些共享我们价值观的国家深入合作，并对那些不共享的国家保持清醒和理性的开放态度。

paragraphs.en: At its core, this means actively taking on the world as it is, not passively waiting for a world we wish to be. It means engaging with the reality that progress is incremental and that interests diverge. It requires recognising the inevitable differences between our countries and others, and then focusing our efforts to work together where we are aligned and where we can make the greatest impact. Progress can be signalled through monologue, but it will be achieved only through dialogue.
paragraphs.zh: 其核心在于主动面对现实世界，而不是被动等待我们希望出现的世界。它意味着要接受现实：进步是渐进的，而利益并不一致。它要求我们承认自己与其他国家之间不可避免的差异，然后把精力集中在双方一致、并且最能产生影响的领域上。进步可以通过单向表态来显示，但只会通过对话来实现。

paragraphs.en: Another reality is that the world is moving from a more multilateral stance to a more multipolar one. Multilateral order, despite its shortcomings, is governed by co-operation and a clear set of rules and norms. A multipolar world, conversely, is one characterised by deals, transactions and chaos. Yet instead of a binary choice between multilateral and multipolar, there is another scenario, one that revolves around plurilateralism. We must strive for a revival of multilateralism with better representation and reformed institutions. At the same time, we should develop plurilateral arrangements.
paragraphs.zh: 另一项现实是，世界正在从更偏重多边主义的格局，转向更具多极特征的格局。尽管不尽完美，多边秩序仍由合作及一套明确的规则和规范来支撑。相反，多极世界则更以交易、协定和混乱为特征。然而，除了在多边与多极之间做二选一，我们还有另一种方案，即以“小多边主义”为核心的路径。我们必须努力复兴更具代表性、并经过改革的多边主义；与此同时，也应当发展“小多边”安排。

paragraphs.en: This approach, a form of variable geometry, will build a dense web of connections—ad hoc coalitions that work, issue by issue. In many cases it will be most effective when dealing with countries with which we disagree on many issues but share enough common ground to act on some. Dynamic, purpose-built coalitions will be increasingly formed around shared interests, rather than a single shared institution. The coalition of the willing now planning Ukraine’s post-war security has become a premier forum for like-minded nations and NATO to co-ordinate efforts. The European Union’s instruments, including the SAFE pact, enable partners to cut through bureaucracy and to build and partner on defence capabilities, at speed. The G7’s critical-minerals alliance is securing and diversifying the supply chains on which every modern economy depends. In AI we could build coalitions to enhance safety and accessibility by leveraging our expertise and capabilities in critical areas such as quantum computing and energy. The question is no longer whether to partner, but how fast we can build successful coalitions that scale across every region.
paragraphs.zh: 这种做法属于“可变几何”形式，将建立一张更密集的网络——按议题运转的临时联盟。很多情况下，它在与许多问题上持不同立场却在某些事项上有足够共识的国家之间最为有效。动态、目标导向的联盟将越来越围绕共享利益组建，而不再依赖单一共同机构。如今，计划乌克兰战后安全架构的“志同道合国家与北约协同”联盟，已经成为同频国家与北约协调行动的主要平台。欧盟的工具，包括SAFE框架，帮助伙伴关系突破官僚障碍，并以更快速度共同建设并整合防务能力。七国集团的关键矿产联盟正在保障并多元化每个现代经济体所依赖的供应链。在人工智能领域，我们也可以围绕量子计算和能源等关键领域的专业能力，建立联盟以提升安全性和可及性。问题不再是是否要合作，而是我们能多快建立出能够跨越所有地区扩展的成功联盟。

paragraphs.en: The answer to shifting orders of geography, trade and commerce—and the anxieties which they understandably cause for citizens—is not to mourn the world that was; it is to build for the world as it is. To build with code, concrete and steel; with institutions that work and strong alliances that protect them. To deepen our countries’ partnerships with as much of the world as we can, clear-eyed and with confidence.
paragraphs.zh: 对于地缘、贸易和商业秩序变化——以及由此给民众带来的、可以理解的不安——的回答，并不是哀悼曾经的世界，而是为现实中的世界而建设。要用代码、混凝土和钢铁去建设；用有效的制度去建设，用能够保护制度的强大联盟去建设。要以清醒而有信心的态度，尽可能深化我们国家与世界各地的伙伴关系。

paragraphs.en: In this effort Canada and Finland can be pivotal, because we are among the most connected economies on Earth through our enviable trade access; powerful, in our capabilities as nations increasing our defence spending to record levels; and purposeful, in our shared commitment to territorial integrity, human rights and pluralism. In a rapidly changing world, we have the grit and the sisu to chart the path ahead—and we know that so have many others whom we invite to join us on this quest.■
paragraphs.zh: 在这一进程中，加拿大和芬兰可能发挥关键作用，因为我们是世界上最具互联性的经济体之一，拥有令人羡慕的贸易通道；在国家能力上，我们将国防开支提升到历史最高水平，实力不容小觑；并且我们有明确目标，致力于维护领土完整、捍卫人权和坚持多元主义。在一个急速变化的世界里，我们拥有开辟前路所需的勇气和“sisu”——那种坚韧不拔的精神——而且我们相信，许多受邀与我们并肩前行的人也同样具备这种品质。■

paragraphs.en: Mark Carney is the prime minister of Canada. Alexander Stubb is the president of Finland.
paragraphs.zh: 马克·卡尼是加拿大总理。亚历山大·斯图布是芬兰总统。
